findings.problem: A recurring reaction formula ("Elsie gave ...") appears four times in high-pressure beats, flattening emotional texture and signaling prose autopilot in exactly the places where scene leverage should feel most distinct.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise reaction beats at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:95, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:217, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:277, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:301 so at least three are rendered through materially different actions (task interruption, breath/voice change, bodily defense, object handling) tied to the immediate stake in that moment (arrears amount, field-call timing, ring-borrow pressure, sobriety skepticism), rather than repeating the same micro-expression template.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if, on direct reading of the revised lines at 95/217/277/301, at least 3 of the 4 beats no longer use the "gave + article + reaction noun" construction and each revised beat adds scene-specific pressure detail that cannot be swapped into another of the four moments without loss of meaning; FAIL if 3 or more beats still share that construction or remain functionally interchangeable.

summary: Chapter-level causality, continuity, tense control, and dialogue register are strong and compliant with the style bible; no MEDIUM+ continuity contradiction, sanitization drift, intimacy fade-to-black, or contraction-level violation was found. However, one unresolved prose tic (repeated "Elsie gave ..." reaction construction in four pressure beats) keeps prose below pass threshold.
